money_flow_summary: Software companies charge recurring fees for tools that automate workflows. In 2026, AI compressed the cost to build SaaS from $500k+ to under $10k — solo founders can build niche B2B tools in days and distribute globally via product-led growth.

opportunities.title: AI Voice Agent for Inbound Business Calls
opportunities.how_money_is_made: Voice AI agent (Vapi/Retell + Claude) answers inbound calls for specific business type, books appointments, answers FAQs, escalates. Charge $199–$999/mo flat or $0.10/min.
opportunities.moat: Business phone system integration creates high switching cost; industry-specific knowledge base improves
opportunities.first_step: Build Vapi agent with dental booking workflow; integrate Google Calendar; cold-call 20 dental offices with live demo
opportunities.ai_agent_angle: Voice AI handles 100% of routine inbound calls 24/7 — answering at 11 PM when the office is closed

competitors.name: Ruby Receptionists
competitors.weakness: Human receptionists at $235–$1,000/mo; limited hours; can't scale; expensive

competitors.name: Smith.ai
competitors.weakness: Human + AI hybrid at $240–$600+/mo; still expensive; not fully automated; slow at scale

competitors.name: Vonage AI
competitors.weakness: Enterprise-focused; complex setup; not vertical-specific; $2k+/mo implementation

opportunities.gap: Ruby and Smith.ai use humans (expensive, limited hours). Vonage is enterprise. The $200–$500/mo fully automated vertical-specific voice agent for SMBs doesn't exist at scale.
poc_steps: Create free Vapi account (no API cost for first 1,000 minutes)
Build a dental appointment booking agent with Google Calendar integration in 1 day
Get a Twilio phone number ($1/mo), forward it to your Vapi agent
Demo live to 5 dental offices — call them, show the agent live

opportunities.title: Vertical AI Agent for Specific SMB Type
opportunities.how_money_is_made: AI agent handles specific SMB's top 3 pain points (e.g., dental: appointment reminders, insurance pre-auth, no-show follow-up). Charge $299–$999/mo. 100 clients = $30k–$100k MRR.
opportunities.moat: Vertical specialization + EHR/practice management integrations create deep lock-in
opportunities.first_step: Interview 5 dental office managers; identify #1 manual task; build MVP in 2 weekends
opportunities.ai_agent_angle: AI makes outbound reminder calls in natural voice, handles patient questions, records outcomes — replacing $25/hr front desk tasks

competitors.name: Weave (dental)
competitors.weakness: $400–$800/mo; limited AI; requires hardware; not truly automated — still needs staff

competitors.name: Podium
competitors.weakness: Messaging platform, not an agent; $250–$500/mo; no automation of actual tasks

competitors.name: Solutionreach
competitors.weakness: Reminder-only; $300+/mo; no AI; no call handling

opportunities.gap: Existing dental software sends reminders but doesn't *do the work* — confirm by phone, handle objections, reschedule cancelled slots automatically. AI agents can.
poc_steps: Pick one function: appointment reminders via phone call
Build Vapi agent that calls patients 24h before appointment, confirms or reschedules
Demo to 1 dental office: 'I'll handle all your reminders for 30 days for free'
Measure no-show reduction; present ROI; charge $299/mo

opportunities.title: AI Document Intelligence (Professional Services)
opportunities.how_money_is_made: Ingests PDF contracts/financial statements/legal docs, extracts structured data (dates, amounts, obligations), surfaces action items. Target law firms, accounting firms at $299–$1,499/mo.
opportunities.moat: Domain-specific prompt templates + integrations with practice management software
opportunities.first_step: Build PDF → structured JSON extraction with Claude; create 3 demo templates (NDA, lease, financial statement)
opportunities.ai_agent_angle: LLM understands context in contracts — identifying obligations that keyword-search tools miss

competitors.name: Ironclad
competitors.weakness: Contract lifecycle management; enterprise only ($50k+/yr); not document extraction; overkill

competitors.name: Kira Systems / Litera
competitors.weakness: Acquisition-due-diligence focused; enterprise; $100k+/yr; not accessible to solo law firms

competitors.name: DocuSign Insight
competitors.weakness: Analytics only; enterprise; requires DocuSign ecosystem; no extraction to actionable items

opportunities.gap: Solo attorneys and small law firms review contracts manually — 30+ min each. Enterprise tools cost $50k+/yr. A $299/mo tool that extracts key clauses and flags obligations for small firms is completely absent.
poc_steps: Download 3 standard NDA templates from the internet
Write Claude prompt: 'extract all obligations, deadlines, and liability clauses from this NDA as JSON'
Build a PDF upload → extraction web page (use PyMuPDF + Flask)
Demo to 3 lawyers: 'analyze any contract in 30 seconds'
